description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 40-43.

Legendary Druid

Even the most bull-headed industrialist will admit that, given the smallest foothold, nature thrives and expands in ways that few other powers in existence can match. And, like mortal armies have their fighters of myth, religions their radiant paladins, and interplanar crime syndicates their unseen rogues, nature finds its greatest exponents in Legendary Druids. Their attunement to the mysterious flow of the natural world is such that their wisdom applies to everything, not just the wilderness they hail from. In the end, every single thing–from the clothes on the backs of mortals and immortals alike, to the food they eat, to even the weapons they wield–comes from their domain.

A Legendary Druid is the ultimate protector of that which comes from nature’s gift. They have spent their life tending what began as seeds and clumps of cells, learning its rhythms, earning its trust, becoming fluent in a language older than most Gods. They have heard things that would shatter a lesser mind–the slow grief of a forest dwindling and dying over centuries, the fury of a storm that remembers when the land was not there, the vast and patient memory of a mountain that has watched civilizations built in its shadow turn to dust. These druids did not break under the strain of knowledge and wisdom. They grew around it the way ancient roots grow over stone, unhurried and quietly, yet terrifyingly permanent.

At 20th level, the Druid is an expression of the natural world–its voice, its memory, its oldest and most patient form of resistance. The power they wield feels like natural consequence. When a Legendary Druid calls down a storm, the sky agrees with their command. When they walk into a blighted land, the dying flowers notice their step and grow anew–or wilt and disappear, becoming nutrients for something new. When these druids speak, ancient things that have remained quiet turn to act, from rocks to roots, to great rivers and forests. Something fundamental accompanies such a paragon of the wild, something that carries, however distantly, the echo of the Great Mother herself: life– violent, unruly, overgrown life, ready to kill and die at their behest. Those who fight next to a Legendary Druid are accompanied by primal forces that they don’t truly understand, but are unvaryingly thankful for.

Form

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Druid table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Cantrips

Prepared

Known

Spells

Legendary Forms

Also at 20th level, you gain the ability to transform into greater forms as part of your wild shape. When you use your Wild Shape, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to turn into any elemental, fey, or monstrosity you have seen before of a Challenge Rating equal to or lower than 10. You transform into an average example of that creature, one without any class levels or the Spellcasting trait. You can’t use any legendary actions, legendary resistances, or lair actions of the new form.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an option of the current step you’re on, jump from one Heroic Path into another.

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and 25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24 using this feature, unless another feature raised your maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Avenger of the Wild

Also at 21st level, your attunement to the wild allows you to call upon natural disasters. As an action, you can expend a Wild Shape use to cast one of the following spells once per day without expending a spell slot: Earthquake, Incendiary Cloud, or Sunburst. Once you use this feature to cast one of these spells, you cannot use it to cast the same spell again until you finish a long rest.

LP Cost. You can use this feature at the end of another creature’s turn by expending 1 Legendary Point.

Level 22

10th-Level Spell

At 22nd level, you learn a single 10th-level spell. You learn an additional 10th-level spell at 24th level. When you start casting a 10th-level spell, you must expend 2 Legendary Points alongside the required action and spell slot.

Verdant Crown

Also at 22nd level, you can use an action to don a Verdant Crown for the next minute, a symbol of absolute authority from your Druid Circle. The crown can be worn while shapeshifted and counts as an 8thlevel spell for the purposes of Tiers of Magic.

While crowned in this way, you can concentrate on an additional spell at a time. If your concentration is broken, all spells you were concentrating on end. The number of spells you can concentrate on at a time can never exceed 3, even if you gain this same benefit from various sources.

In addition, your natural glory causes Beasts and Plants of CR 15 or lower within 120 feet of you to be completely enthralled. They are friendly to you, can understand you irrespective of what language you speak, and will heed all commands issued by you, unless they are directly harmful to them.

Improved Legendary Forms

Also at 22nd level, the Challenge Rating limit of your Legendary Forms is increased to 15.

5.5e: You gain an additional use of your Wild Shape feature. When you reach 24th level, you gain an additional use.

Level 23

Extraplanar Forms

Beginning at 23rd level, you gain the ability to transform into greater forms as part of your wild shape. When you use your Wild Shape, you can expend 2 Legendary Points to turn into any aberration, celestial, or fiend you have seen before of a Challenge Rating of 19 or lower. You transform into an average example of that creature, one without any class levels or the Spellcasting trait. You can’t use any legendary actions, legendary resistances, or lair actions of the new form.

Level 24

Archdruid’s Resilience

When you reach 24th level, you become resistant to cold and fire damage, both in your true form and in your Wild Shape. Additionally, whenever you finish a long rest, choose one other damage type among the following: acid, lightning, or thunder. You become resistant to that damage type in all your forms until you choose another type upon finishing a long rest.

Rejuvenating Recovery

Also at 24th level, when you use the Recover action, you regain a use of your Wild Shape feature and are cleansed of one harmful condition or magical effect of 7th level or lower currently afflicting you.

Level 25

Final Form

At 25th level, your wild shape mastery makes you able to replicate even the most exotic and unnatural lifeforms. When you use your Wild Shape, you can choose to expend 3 Legendary Points to turn into any creature that isn’t a Construct that you have seen before, of a Challenge Rating equal to or lower than your Druid level. You transform into an average example of that creature, one without any class levels or the Spellcasting trait. You can’t use any legendary actions, legendary resistances, or lair actions of the new form.

5.5e: Additionally, when you convert uses of Wild Shape into a spell slot, each use of your Wild Shape contributes to 3 spell levels. For example, if you convert two uses of Wild Shape, you produce a 6th-level spell slot. You cannot use this feature to gain a 10th-level spell slot. Once you use this benefit, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
